elements.sent-2411-001: We consider the fundamental problem of learning the parameters of an undirected graphical model or Markov Random Field (MRF) in the setting where the edge weights are chosen at random.
elements.sent-2411-002: For Ising models, we show that a multiplicative-weight update algorithm due to Klivans and Meka learns the parameters in polynomial time for any inverse temperature $\beta \leq \sqrt{\log n}$.
elements.sent-2411-003: This immediately yields an algorithm for learning the Sherrington-Kirkpatrick (SK) model beyond the high-temperature regime of $\beta < 1$.
elements.sent-2411-004: Prior work breaks down at $\beta = 1$ and requires heavy machinery from statistical physics or functional inequalities.
elements.sent-2411-005: In contrast, our analysis is relatively simple and uses only subgaussian concentration.
elements.sent-2411-006: Our results extend to MRFs of higher order (such as pure $p$-spin models), where even results in the high-temperature regime were not known.
elements.sent-2411-007: In this work, we revisit the problem of structure learning and parameter recovery in undirected graphical models or Markov Random Fields (MRFs) over a binary alphabet.
elements.sent-2411-008: This class of distributions plays a significant role in various fields, including statistical physics, mathematics, and theoretical computer science.
elements.sent-2411-009: Perhaps the simplest and most well-studied class of graphical models is the Ising model.
elements.sent-2411-010: An Ising model $D_{A,h}$ is a distribution over $\cube{n}$ given by the factorization \begin{equation}\Pr_{X\sim D_{A,h}}[X=x]\propto \exp\Biggr(\sum_{i<j}A_{ij}x_ix_j+\sum_{i=1}^{n}h_ix_i\Biggr).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-011: Here $A$ is called the interaction interaction matrix and $h$ is the external field.
elements.sent-2411-012: The dependency graph of $D_{A,h}$ is the set of edges $(i,j)$ for which $A_{ij}$ is non zero.
elements.sent-2411-013: The {\em structure learning problem is to recover the underlying dependency graph $G$ given only iid samples from $D_{A,h}$.}
elements.sent-2411-014: We refer to the more difficult problem of recovering the edges of $G$ and their associated weights as {\em parameter recovery .}
elements.sent-2411-015: The most commonly studied complexity measure for structure learning is the {\em width of the Ising model denoted $\lambda(A,h) = \max_{i (\sum_j |A_{ij | + |h_i|$).}}}
elements.sent-2411-016: Klivans and Meka \cite{sparsitron obtained the first algorithm with nearly optimal sample complexity and running time for learning Ising models (see also \cite{Bresler15,VuffrayMLC16,hamilton2017information ).}}
elements.sent-2411-017: Their multiplicative-weight update algorithm {\em Sparsitron uses $N = \exp(O(\lambda))\log n$ samples, runs in time $O(n^{2 N)$, and additionally recovers the parameters of the graph.}}
